nodes.name: Uggi A
nodes.description: The primary star of the Uggi system, Uggi A is an orange dwarf, smaller and cooler than Earth's sun, but expected to burn for billions of years after Sol is no more. It also has significantly more magnetic activity and solar flares than a yellow sun like Sol.

nodes.name: Uggi B
nodes.description:
Too distant from the main Uggi system to use an M-drive without interruption, and with only a single satellite, this red dwarf companion to Uggi A is effectively an almost entirely separate - and uncolonized - system. While colonization is certainly possible, the lack of significant quantities of readily accessible minerals in this neck of the system makes any large constructions challenging.

As a red dwarf, Uggi B is subject to extremely high levels of magnetic activity, solar flares, and even super solar flares that could be dangerous to those subject to them.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Likely <1 kyr before major orbital disruption (collision/ejection/infall risk). Drivers: Minor-body crossing in pair Uggi B/Barry; Critical Hill spacing (Delta=0.40).

nodes.name: Adonis
nodes.description: A beautiful gas giant with broad, thick rings, Adonis would likely be a good source of both fuel and water, if it were not so distant from those that might need it.

nodes.name: Janus
nodes.description: Janus is a tidally locked, rocky planet very close to the star Uggi A. The side that faces the star is extremely hot, while the side facing away is extremely cold. The atmosphere of the planet, if there ever was one, was blasted away millions of years ago by the intense stellar radiation.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Marginal Hill spacing (Delta=6.01).

nodes.name: Fuller
nodes.description: The primary source of fuel for the Uggi system, Fuller is an immense hydrogen-rich gas giant.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Likely 1-100 Myr before disruption under sustained perturbations. Drivers: Marginal Hill spacing (Delta=6.01); Tight Hill spacing (Delta=3.94).

nodes.name: Uggi (Main World)

traveller.name: Uggi
traveller.raw: Spic	J	0930	Uggi	E58AAFC-C		Hi Wa Pz Ht	A	703	SoCf	K0 V M9 V	{ 1 }	(F9C+4)	[DB8F]		9	6480
traveller.exDesc: Resources: [F] Very Abundant, Labor: [9] Pop 10^9, Infra: [C] Extensive, Efficiency: +4
traveller.cxDesc: Heterogeneity: [D] Discordant, Acceptance: [B] Very Xenophilic, Strangeness: [8] Confusing, Symbols: [F] Extremely Abstract

nodes.description:
The mainworld of the Uggi system, planet Uggi is home to nearly 70 billion humans.

Due to its tight orbit, Uggi is more than 30MM km inside the jump shadow of its star, requiring significant time and energy to go between the planet and the nearest jump zone.

***

Time to/from Jump Zone:

1G: 32.4 hours
2G: 22.9 hours
3G: 18.7 hours
4G: 16.2 hours
5G: 14.5 hours
6G: 13.3 hours
7G: 12.3 hours
8G: 11.5 hours
9G: 10.9 hours
10G: 10.3 hours

*Passenger Transport*
Passenger Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): DM+4 (due to incredibly high population and tourism)
Quick Examples:
- a single human male seeks middle passage to the Nursian Monastery to begin a personal retreat
- a team of six wannabe human belters seek basic passage to any asteroid-based settlement in the Dust Belt
- a pair of elderly tourists, husband and wife, are seeking immediate high passage to Ravenwood or any system on the way there

*Freight Transport*
Freight Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): DM+2 (due to incredibly high population)
Quick Examples:
- 20 tons of high-grade fertilizer needs delivery to Agricorp Orbital Food Fab in the next 7 days
- 50 tons of polymers (kelp-based) needs delivery to MechaCorp Shipyard in the next 14 days
- 1 ton of electronics (portable computers and electronic reading pads) needs delivery to the Nursian Monastery in the next 30 days

*Speculative Trade*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None (Despite Uggi's huge population, it is surprisingly self-sufficient and both supply and demand are unmodified outside of the Core rules.)
Quick Examples of Sellers:
- offering 200 tons of common consumables (kelp paste)
- offering 5 tons of gems (pearls)
- offering 20 tons of polymers (kelp-based plastics)

Quick Examples of Purchasers:
- seeking 200 tons of common ore (iron and nickel)
- seeking 5 tons of luxury goods (any)
- seeking 50 tons of uncommon raw materials (titanium, etc.)
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Likely 1-100 Myr before disruption under sustained perturbations. Drivers: Tight Hill spacing (Delta=3.94); Marginal Hill spacing (Delta=7.87).

nodes.name: Gastalt
nodes.description: Roughly half the mass of Fuller, Gastalt is nonetheless a critical part of the Uggian economy. It serves as the primary source of fuel between Uggi and the Dust Belt, feeding fuel depots along its entire orbit. While mainly being a source of hydrogen, Gastalt could also provide oxygen from the ice that forms its broad ring.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Marginal Hill spacing (Delta=7.87).

nodes.name: Dust Belt
nodes.description:
The Dust Belt is a relatively typical belt of dust, sparse asteroids, and a few planetoids, mostly composed of water ice, nickel-iron, or silica.  The areas of the belt with the highest concentrations of each of these resources are home to one or more settlements focused on their processing and distribution. These settlements are built in hollowed out asteroids, which support a few large extraction teams and numerous smaller ones all operating nearby.

The population of the Dust Belt, colloquially known as Belters, numbers in the hundreds of thousands - relatively few for a system with an overall population as high as Uggi.

The Dust Belt and everything inside it is colloquially known as the "Inner System". Beyond it is the "Outer System".

nodes.name: Diamedes
nodes.description:
Diamedes is large for a moon, nearly the size of the Earth, though it is denser with a surface gravity of 1.52G. It is tidally locked to its planet, meaning that it completes a full revolution only once every 69.7 standard days. Its surface is mostly covered in water, which freezes throughout its long nights before again thawing under persistent sunlight.

For a few hours every orbit, the entire surface of Diamedes plunges into Gastalt's shadow for several hours, as the gas giant eclipses the sun. During that time, even the daylit side grows dark and temperatures drop several degrees, before warming again once the eclipse passes.

While nominally outside the system's habitable zone, the side of Diamedes facing Uggi A is actually quite temperate - averaging a pleasant 22° Celcius (72° F). This is due to constant direct sunlight striking the same area on the moon's surface for a prolonged period, over a standard month, as its rotation is quite slow, with little in the way of wind to steal away the warmth.

The moon also boasts a roughly Earth-like atmosphere, requiring only occasional supplemental oxygen. As a result Diamedes boasts a handful of human settlements consisting of solar-powered airships that continuously tool along at roughly 20km/hr., circumnavigating the globe at the same speed as its rotation, thus remaining in perpetual daylight.

These hardy and adventurous souls take from any number of native species of birds that only touch land to nest, as well as the eggs the birds lay. They also periodically drag nets in the ocean to catch fish imported from other worlds, or native fish-equivalents. These goods, as well as clear, hard gizzard stones left by some of the moon's creatures, they sometimes trade to the crews of passing ships, in exchange for machinery, parts, and other necessities that they cannot easily provide for themselves.

Legend has it that one of the original surveyors of the Uggi system made a typo and that this moon was meant to be named Diomedes, after a great hero of an ancient war. Some say the surveyor was distracted by the discovery of diamonds on some of the peaks jutting out of the moon's waters, which in turn has resulted in intermittent waves of prospectors coming over the centuries without any public acknowledgement of riches being found.

Regardless, if it was a typo, no one has corrected it in the millennia since it was made.

***

Time to/from Jump Zone:

1G: 12.5 hours
2G: 8.8 hours
3G: 7.2 hours
4G: 6.3 hours
5G: 5.6 hours
6G: 5.1 hours
7G: 4.7 hours
8G: 4.4 hours
9G: 4.2 hours
10G: 4.0 hours

*Passenger Transport*
Passenger Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): DM-2 (due to lack of starport and diffuse population)
Quick Examples:
- a single human female seeks basic passage to anywhere to begin a new life
- a single human male seeks basic passage to Gastalt Gas & Go after being abandoned on Diamedes by "friends" as a prank
- a pair of young human tourist adventurers are seeking standard passage to anywhere to start their next adventure

*Freight Transport*
Freight Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): DM-2 (due to lack of starport and diffuse population)
Quick Examples:
- 5 tons of gems (gizzard stones) needs delivery to HyperLight Inc. Laser Forge in the next 30 days
- 10 tons of luxury consumables (Diamedian giant glider eggs) needs refrigerated delivery to Uggi in the next 7 days
- 1 ton of live animals (a pair of Diamedian eels) needs delivery to Uggi in the next 14 days

*Speculative Trade*
DM-2 (due to lack of starport and diffuse population)
Quick Examples of Sellers:
- offering 50 tons of common consumables (fish equivalents)
- offering 1 ton of gems (gizzard stones)
- offering 1 ton of live animals (Diamedian giant gliders)

Quick Examples of Purchasers:
- seeking 10 tons of common electronics (solar panels, portable computers, etc.)
- seeking 5 tons of wood (any)
- seeking 50 tons of textiles (any clothing and fabrics)

nodes.name: Buffalo Base
nodes.description:
A major habitat and station built *inside* a hollowed-out asteroid, using the asteroid's mass for radiation shielding and spinning it for gravity. No main propulsion. Primary facilities includes refining capabilities for water ice (found in huge subsurface deposits covered in a layer of regolith) and conversion to refined fuel. The population is approximately 5k.

***

Time to/from Jump Zone: < 1 hour

*Passenger Transport*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples:
- a single elderly human female seeks standard passage to Uggi to begin her retirement and gain access to better medical care
- a team of four human belters seek basic passage to Taiko Water Co Extraction Team Alpha for work
- a trio of human executives seeks high passage to Energettica Inc. Battery Fab to finalize negotiations for a business deal

*Freight Transport*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples:
- 100 tons of common raw materials (refined fuel, hydrogen) needs delivery to Silicaceous Beneficiator (SilBen) in the next 7 days
- 50 tons of common raw materials (oxygen) needs delivery to NiFe Town in the next 14 days
- 30 tons of common raw materials (water) needs delivery to AgriCorp Orbital Food Fab in the next 7 days

*Speculative Trade*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples of Sellers:
- offering 200 tons of common raw materials (refined fuel, hydrogen)
- offering 400 tons of common raw materials (oxygen)
- offering 20 tons of common raw materials (water)

Quick Examples of Purchasers:
- seeking 100 tons of common consumables (food)
- seeking 50 tons of common industrial goods (machine components and spare parts)
- seeking 30 tons of common manufactured goods (household appliances)

nodes.name: Bergamen
nodes.description: Bergamen is a relatively small gas giant, with broad rings that are so thin and faint as to be nearly invisible to the human eye. It has no settlements and little in the way of economic value, except as a source of hydrogen that is too distant from most places of interest to be economically interesting. Should the outer system ever be more heavily populated, interest in Bergamen would likely increase.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Minor-body crossing in pair Bergamen/Aletes.

nodes.name: Gastalt Ring A
nodes.description: This icy ring is both broad and thick, potentially providing a source of water. While some companies have discussed creating a cracking and distribution facility within the rings to offer hydrogen and oxygen, the existing ready access to hydrogen from Gastalt itself makes the prospect less interesting.

nodes.name: Bergamen Ring B
nodes.description: The middle ring, Bergamen Ring B is composed primarily of water ice. It is generally considered too thin for economical extraction.

nodes.name: Bergamen Ring A
nodes.description: The inner ring, Bergamen Ring A is composed primarily of organics and silicates. It is generally considered too thin for economical extraction.

nodes.name: Bergamen Ring C
nodes.description: Bergamen Ring C, the outer ring, is the rockiest, composed primarily of dust. Like its siblings it is generally considered too thin for economical extraction.

nodes.name: Hades
nodes.description: Hades is a large icy planet, about two and a half times the mass of Earth, with a thin atmosphere consisting mostly nitrogen and methane. It has no population and negligible economic value.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Minor-body crossing in pair Aletes/Hades.

nodes.name: Xygax
nodes.description:
This massive gas giant is nearly a brown dwarf.

While it technically sits outside the M-drive friendly volume provided by Uggi A, its own mass is large enough to support M-drives out to an extent that reaches Uggi A's own ability to support them. As a result, it is possible (just barely) to travel from the inner system to Xygax without any disruption in M-drive capabilities. As a result of this interesting navigational topology, Xygax is home to an orbiting research outpost studying the manufacturing of more advanced anti-gravity technologies, which can only be conducted in nano-gravity environments outside of M-drive zones.

nodes.name: Barry
nodes.description:
Unlike most other bodies in the Uggi system, Barry shares its orbit between both Uggi A and Uggi B, orbiting the barycenter of the two stars. It is a frigid, rocky super-Earth too cold and too distant to be of interest economically.

Those that wish to visit Barry would need to coast most of the way there, as it is far outside any M-drive zone, and at a relatively low velocity to allow enough time for M-drive braking once its own relatively tiny gravity well has been reached.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Likely <1 kyr before major orbital disruption (collision/ejection/infall risk). Drivers: Minor-body crossing in pair Uggi B/Barry; Critical Hill spacing (Delta=0.40); Orbit overlap in pair Barry/Philonius.

nodes.name: Philonius
nodes.description: The most distant world in the Uggi system, and less than half the size of Earth, Philonius would take months to reach even at its closest approach due to the lack of M-drive availability during most of the journey and the relatively small gravity well that could be used for M-drive braking once the planet was reached.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Likely <1 kyr before major orbital disruption (collision/ejection/infall risk). Drivers: Orbit overlap in pair Barry/Philonius.

nodes.name: Highport (Abandoned)
nodes.description:
This unnamed structure is a partially constructed highport, meant to serve as the primary port and refueling depot for traffic between Planet Uggi and the broader system. Construction was abandoned 15 years ago due to cost overruns and changes in government.

The structure still receives periodic fuel deliveries to power its grav plates, due to multi-decade contracts made when it was still under construction. There are rumors of corruption related to these contracts. If and when fueling ceases, the highport will need to be moved to an orbit that is naturally stable, unlike its current artificially low orbit, which requires ongoing thrust to maintain.

[Note: the on-map representation is inaccurate. The structure is in low geosynchronous orbit immediately below the UggiSec Commsat and immediately above the capital city of Lucra Causa. This orbit is not natural, as it requires constant thrust from grav plates to maintain the position of the highport, and this tool doesn't allow representation of such altered orbits.]

nodes.name: Radzen
nodes.description:
Fuller's only moon, Radzen would potentially be habitable were it not for the immense tidal forces caused by the gravitational tug of war between its enormous planet and the star that both orbit. These tidal forces cause immense heat within the core of the planet, leaving it geologically unstable and its surface blistering hot. It has no atmosphere to speak of.

Some have pointed out that Radzen's geological instability results in periodic exposure or even outcroppings of rare and valuable minerals. However, none have yet attempted to overcome the inhospitality of the moon to act upon that opportunity.

nodes.name: UggiSec Commsat Prime (Debris)
nodes.description: A large communications satellite in geosynchronous orbit, believed by the FFU to be the primary communications node for UggiSec's global surveillance system.

nodes.name: Janus L4 Stellar Research Outpost
nodes.description:
A small, rotating habitation module designed for long-term scientific observation of Uggi A, with a focus on monitoring the star's frequent stellar flares. Includes sensor arrays and a small centrifuge for gravity.

All human-habitable sections are heavily shielded from the immense radiation present so close to the star.

nodes.name: HydraCorp Refined Fuel Depot
nodes.description: A bustling, rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as a primary market and refueling depot for system traffic. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.

nodes.name: Silicaceous Beneficiator (SilBen)
nodes.description:
A major habitat and station built *inside* a hollowed-out asteroid, using the asteroid's mass for radiation shielding and spinning it for gravity. No main propulsion. Primary facilities includes refining capabilities for silica ores as well as oxygen capture and storage. The population is approximately 15k.

***

Time to/from Jump Zone: < 1 hour

*Passenger Transport*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples:
- a human couple with a baby seeks standard passage to Uggi to introduce their family to the new child
- a team of three human belters seek basic passage to Sandman Extraction Team Beta for work
- a trio of human business executives seeks high passage to NiFe Town to discuss a partnership to build an orbital chip plant

*Freight Transport*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples:
- 100 tons of common raw materials (silica) needs delivery to Uggi in the next 7 days
- 50 tons of common raw materials (oxygen) needs delivery to NiFe Town in the next 7 days
- 30 tons of common raw materials (silica) needs delivery to Uggi in the next 14 days

*Speculative Trade*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples of Sellers:
- offering 200 tons of common raw materials (silica)
- offering 400 tons of common raw materials (oxygen)
- offering 20 tons of common raw materials (water)

Quick Examples of Purchasers:
- seeking 100 tons of common consumables (food)
- seeking 50 tons of common industrial goods (machine components and spare parts)
- seeking 30 tons of common manufactured goods (household appliances)

nodes.name: Gastalt Gas & Go
nodes.description: A bustling, rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as a primary market and refueling depot for system traffic. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.

nodes.name: NiFeTown
nodes.description:
A major habitat and station built *inside* a hollowed-out asteroid, using the asteroid's mass for radiation shielding and spinning it for gravity. No main propulsion. Primary facilities includes refining capabilities for nickel and iron ores. The largest settlement in the Dust Belt, the population is approximately 35k.

***

Time to/from Jump Zone: < 1 hour

*Passenger Transport*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples:
- a single young human male seeks standard passage to SilBen after a failed relationship
- a team of a dozen human belters seek basic passage to IronMan Inc. Extraction Team Alpha for work
- a pair of human business executives seeks high passage to MechaCorp Shipyard to discuss extending a contract

*Freight Transport*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples:
- 200 tons of common raw materials (iron) needs delivery to Uggi in the next 7 days
- 50 tons of common raw materials (nickel) needs delivery to Uggi in the next 14 days
- 30 tons of common raw materials (nickel) needs delivery to MechaCorp Shipyard in the next 3 days

*Speculative Trade*
Modifiers (in addition to Core Rules): None
Quick Examples of Sellers:
- offering 200 tons of common raw materials (iron)
- offering 100 tons of common raw materials (nickel)
- offering 40 tons of uncommon raw materials (cadmium)

Quick Examples of Purchasers:
- seeking 200 tons of common consumables (food)
- seeking 100 tons of common industrial goods (machine components and spare parts)
- seeking 80 tons of common manufactured goods (household appliances)

nodes.name: ChillCorp Fuel Depot
nodes.description: A bustling, rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as a primary market and refueling depot for system traffic. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.

nodes.name: Cerebus Alpha
nodes.description: Unlike its frigid parent, Cerebus Alpha is extremely hot with a churning, molten surface. This is caused by extreme tidal forces due to the large mass of the moon and its extremely close orbit to Hades, which constantly stretch and rend the moon's core, causing immense friction sufficient to keep the moon in a semi-molten state.

nodes.name: Cerebus Beta
nodes.description: Cerebus Beta has wildly varying temperatures on its surface, caused in part by tidal forces from Hades and Cerebus Alpha and less so by its slow rotation, which allows warmth from the sun to accumulate. It has no atmosphere.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Marginal Hill spacing (Delta=6.89).

nodes.name: Cerebus Gamma
nodes.description: This small and distant satellite of Hades is frigid, lacking an atmosphere or any trappings of habitability.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Marginal Hill spacing (Delta=6.89).

nodes.name: Macula
nodes.description: This small frozen moon is, at times, the most distant body from Uggi reachable by uninterrupted M-drive. It has no atmosphere and no settlements.

nodes.name: Adonis Ring A
nodes.description: This broad, thick ring is composed primarily of ice near the inside and rocks near the outside. It would be a good source of oxygen, while also providing limited building materials if this side of the system were ever colonized.

nodes.name: Inevitable - Midu Agashaam Class Destroyer
nodes.description: A 3000 dTon Midu Agashaam class destroyer, with a skeleton crew in warm storage. This ship can respond to immediate local threats, but is not crewed or supplied for interstellar missions, a task which would likely require up to a week to complete.
nodes.gmNotes:
The ship has readied the following missile loadout:

* 4 triple turrets with standard missiles (144 standard missiles)
* 1 triple turret with long range missiles (36 long range missiles)
* 1 triple turret with anti-torpedo missiles (36 anti-torpedo missiles)

Each turret can fire salvos of 3 missiles at a time.

Plus the following missiles in storage:
* 216 standard missiles
* 36 long range missiles
* 36 anti-torpedo missiles

nodes.name: Uggi L4 Deep Space Sensor Platform
nodes.description: An unmanned stationary platform boasting an array of sensors for detecting, tracking, and identifying deep space objects, as well as tight-beam communications systems for sending and receiving data over long distances.

nodes.name: Uggi L5 Deep Space Sensor Platform (Debris)
nodes.description: An unmanned stationary platform boasting an array of sensors for detecting, tracking, and identifying deep space objects, as well as tight-beam communications systems for sending and receiving data over long distances.

nodes.name: Fuller L4 Deep Space Sensor Platform
nodes.description: An unmanned stationary platform boasting an array of sensors for detecting, tracking, and identifying deep space objects, as well as tight-beam communications systems for sending and receiving data over long distances.

nodes.name: Fuller L5 Deep Space Sensor Platform (Debris)
nodes.description: An unmanned stationary platform boasting an array of sensors for detecting, tracking, and identifying deep space objects, as well as tight-beam communications systems for sending and receiving data over long distances.

nodes.name: UggiSec Sensor Platform
nodes.description:
The mainworld of the Uggi system, planet Uggi is home to nearly 70 billion human sophonts.

Due to its tight orbit, Uggi is 30MM km within the jump shadow of its star, requiring significant time and energy to go between the planet and the nearest jump zone.

Time to/from Jump Zone:

1G: 32.4 hours
2G: 22.9 hours
3G: 18.7 hours
4G: 16.2 hours
5G: 14.5 hours
6G: 13.3 hours
7G: 12.3 hours
8G: 11.5 hours
9G: 10.9 hours
10G: 10.3 hours
nodes.gmNotes:
While presenting itself as a humble sensor platform, this platform also holds a small missile bay containing 144 missiles with the following loadout:
* 12 anti-torpedo missiles (Thrust 15, 1D damage, Distant range)
* 12 fragmentation missiles (Thrust 15, 3D damage, Distant range)
* 60 long-range missiles (Thrust 15, 3D damage, Very Distant range)
* 60 standard missiles (Thrust 10, 4D damage, Distant range)

It can fire salvos of 12 missiles at a time.

nodes.name: Taiko Water Co Extraction Team Alpha
nodes.description: A manned, mobile platform specialising in asteroid resource extraction, usually nickel and iron, silica, or ice.

nodes.name: Taiko Water Co Extraction Team Beta
nodes.description: A manned, mobile platform specialising in asteroid resource extraction, usually nickel and iron, silica, or ice.

nodes.name: Sandman Extraction Team Alpha
nodes.description: A manned, mobile platform specialising in asteroid resource extraction, usually nickel and iron, silica, or ice.

nodes.name: Sandman Extraction Team Beta
nodes.description: A manned, mobile platform specialising in asteroid resource extraction, usually nickel and iron, silica, or ice.

nodes.name: NiCo Extraction Team Alpha
nodes.description: A manned, mobile platform specialising in asteroid resource extraction, usually nickel and iron, silica, or ice.

nodes.name: IronMan Inc. Extraction Team Alpha
nodes.description: A manned, mobile platform specialising in asteroid resource extraction, usually nickel and iron, silica, or ice.

nodes.name: CrackerCorp H & O2 Depot Gamma
nodes.description:
A bustling, rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as a primary market and refueling depot for system traffic. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.

CrackerCorp runs a number of monopoly-busting stations throughout the Dust Belt. They position their facilities along the same orbit, and as close as possible, to the main facilities in the area, barely respecting the legally mandated 100km exclusion zone around most interplanetary structures of a certain size. They then run streamlined operations focused on gathering water ice, cracking it into hydrogen and oxygen (O2), and providing low-touch filling services at prices slightly less than the facility they are shadowing.

With CrackerCorp's laser-like focus on doing just one thing really, extremely efficiently, the neighboring stations occasionally find it useful to buy fuel from them, especially if their internally generated supplies are disrupted or otherwise insufficient.

"CrackerCorp - we crack water AND monoplies!"

nodes.name: Maxim Inc. High Compute Array
nodes.description:
A massive array of photonic panels with integrated heat sinks, optimized to maximize energy production while minimizing thermal retention. At its heart is an optionally-manned platform filled with high-compute systems and communications arrays.

The array and attached compute is owned by Maxim Inc., which leases high compute cycles to those with high compute needs. Data is usually compressed and encrypted before being tight-beamed to and from the array, however particularly sensitive or large data sets may on occasion need to be delivered by hand.

A single docking port and tiny crew quarters can support up to two crew members for up to a week as needed for maintenance or specific mission requirements. All human-habitable sections are heavily shielded from the immense radiation present so close to the star.

nodes.name: CrackerCorp H & O2 Depot Alpha
nodes.description:
A bustling, rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as a primary market and refueling depot for system traffic. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.

CrackerCorp runs a number of monopoly-busting stations throughout the Dust Belt. "CrackerCorp - we crack water AND monoplies!"

nodes.name: Advanced Gravitics Inc. Nano-Gravity Research Outpost
nodes.description:
A small, rotating habitation module designed for long-term scientific observation. Includes sensor arrays and a small centrifuge for gravity.

Orbiting less than half an AU from the edge of the M-drive zone, the research team periodically launches a research ship containing numerous tests towards Uggi A. While outside the M-drive zone, the ship loses M-drive capabilities for a time, drifting without acceleration, which allows nano-gravity experiments to be conducted. Once the ship re-enters the M-drive zone created by Uggi A's gravity well, it turns around and conducts another pass back towards the research outpost, conducting more experiments.

nodes.name: Aletes
nodes.description:
This planetoid is almost certainly a relatively new - in astronomical terms - addition to the Uggi system, as its orbit is not only highly eccentric, and with an unusual degree of inclination, but retrograde to the rest of the worlds, indicating it originated outside the initial matter disk from which the Uggi system evolved. It was likely an interstellar vagrant, captured by Uggi A within the last few hundred million years. Thankfully, it has yet to cause any major disruption to the system.

It is composed of water ice surrounding a rocky core. As a result of this, as well as its erratic orbit and thus unstable geology, it has yet to be settled or mined, though some wonder if its alien origins could mean it contains elements rare to the Uggi system, such as precious metals.
nodes.orbitalStabilityDetails: Metastable; generally long-lived (>100 Myr to Gyr), but dynamically packed and perturbation-sensitive. Drivers: Minor-body crossing in pair Bergamen/Aletes; Minor-body crossing in pair Aletes/Hades.

nodes.name: MechaCorp Shipyard
nodes.description: Despite its name, this MechaCorp facility constructs small platforms, habitats, station modules, and accessories in addition to a variety of small ships. It is limited to TL12 constructs of no more than 400 dTon. It is a pure fabrication facility, leaving repairs and upgrades for others to do, and is one of the primary consumers of nickel and iron from the Dust Belt.

nodes.name: Raley's Repairs & Upgrades
nodes.description:
Raley's is known as the best and most affordable shop in the Uggi system for repairs and upgrades.

It's also the only one.

nodes.name: Nursian Monastery
nodes.description:
A small, rotating module designed for long-term habitation. Includes a small centrifuge for gravity.

This habitat is a dedicated monastery owned by the Catholic Church. A dozen Benedictine monks spend their days here in quiet meditation and contemplation, writing treatises on various subjects of religion and faith. They grow their own food in an agricultural module tended by the monks and facilitate periodic retreats for laypersons where they teach classes on both familiar and esoteric topics. Two of the monks are physicians and a small medical bay provides medical services for those that need it, allowing them to accept visitors who are sick or elderly.

Visitors are always welcomed by the monks, though prior notice is greatly appreciated.

nodes.name: Goldrush Mine (Abandoned)
nodes.description: Over a thousand years ago this ancient mine was drilled into an asteroid to reach rare and precious minerals deep in the interior. For a time it was then used as a habitat while various mining crews sought similar treasures in the asteroids near it. At some point, all such attempts were abandoned, as was the mine itself. It now hurls silently through the Dust Belt, stripped of anything of value, a testimony to efforts long past.
nodes.gmNotes: This abandoned location is listed as a potential hideout for the FFU, should they need a secret refuge in future.

nodes.name: Adonis Orbital Research Outpost
nodes.description: This small facility is hidden amongst the larger pieces of debris that make up the Adonis Ring.
nodes.gmNotes: While any who discover it and inquire will be told that it is a research facility studying the Adonis Ring, in actuality this is the FFU's most distant base of operations. It was provided, and its ongoing operation is funded, by a mysterious group from outside the Uggi system. The main intermediary between the FFU and this group is Father Felix, who alone may know its identity and motive.

nodes.name: Energettica Inc. Battery Fab
nodes.description:
A major habitat and station built *inside* a hollowed-out asteroid, using the asteroid's mass for radiation shielding and spinning it for gravity. No main propulsion.

The Energettica Inc. Battery Fab is a combined ore mine, fuel processor, and factory all built into a single, large asteroid containing diverse resources.

nodes.name: AgriCorp Orbital Food Fab
nodes.description:
A huge, rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as a primary source of agricultural products outside of the Uggi gravity well. Planting, harvesting, and processing is highly automated, overseen by a staff of a few hundred astroculture specialists. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.

AgriCorp keeps its prices low, both to compete with food coming from Uggi as well as to deter potential future orbital competition.

nodes.name: Glitter Belt
nodes.description: The remnants of a failed protoplanet ripped to shreds by the Xygax's gravity, the Glitter Belt is comprised of denser materials than the inner Dust Belt, including minerals and precious metals, as well as water ice, whose sparkle gave the belt its name. The sparsity of those materials, the distance from the system's population centers, and the fact that only the inner reaches of the ring are within M-drive range all hinder broader exploitation.

nodes.name: ThrustMax Engine Fab
nodes.description: Positioned within a couple hundred kilometers of the MechaCorp Shipyard, this facility builds engines and engine parts for M-drives.

nodes.name: HyperLight Inc. Laser Forge
nodes.description:
A small, rotating habitation module attached to a fabrication facility primarily equipped to manufacture laser mining drills.

This is a very small operation, whose work is in part fed by the slow, steady demand for new laser mining drills. The team here also takes on custom fabrication jobs for a variety of tools and equipment used by belters, including laser welders, portable laser drills, range finders, laser transceivers, lidar sensors, medical lasers, and the like.

nodes.name: H & O Depot (Abandoned)
nodes.description:
A small, rotating habitation module attached to storage and refueling facilities. It was build during a period of economic expansion across the Uggi system, when its owners bet on being the first to provide refined fuel and oxygen to the Outer System. That bet didn't pay off as development slowed and the depot ended up going largely unused by customers.

It now orbits Bergamen, abandoned for decades. Its orbit is unstable and slowly expanding, due to escape Bergamen's gravity entirely within a few thousand years.
nodes.gmNotes: This abandoned location is listed as a potential hideout for the FFU, should they need a secret refuge in future.

nodes.name: Water Well (Abandoned)
nodes.description:
A major habitat and station built *inside* a hollowed-out asteroid, using the asteroid's mass for radiation shielding and spinning it for gravity. No main propulsion.

This facility was built over an eon ago in a rocky asteroid containing huge deposits of water ice, which were covered in a layer of regolith that protected the ice from direct sunlight and the resulting sublimation. It was a successful operation for over two centuries. However, as a result of actions by a rival corporation, the regolith layer was blasted off, exposing the ice to direct sunlight, causing it to quickly vaporize within a number of years. The loss of value in the form of water ice, not to mention related and unpredictable disruptions to its orbital path, led to the base being abandoned.

With nearly all water ice gone, the asteroid now remains a rocky shell, offering protection from radiation, but with no economic value to speak of. If orbital mechanics should ever bring it close to asteroids with minerals or water, it might again be occupied, at least temporarily, but that seems unlikely in the near future...especially as most have forgotten its existence.
nodes.gmNotes: This abandoned location is listed as a potential hideout for the FFU, should they need a secret refuge in future.

nodes.name: Glitter Gulch (Abandoned)
nodes.description:
A major habitat and station built *inside* a hollowed-out asteroid, using the asteroid's mass for radiation shielding and spinning it for gravity. No main propulsion.

Glitter Gulch, so called because of its position deep within a ravine on the surface of an asteroid, was the preeminent location for mining platinum in the Glitter Belt. It was abandoned over 1200 years ago once the platinum in the asteroid, and those nearby, ran out.

Despite delivering a significant volume of platinum - possibly still the majority of that in use on planet Uggi - the operation was never very profitable due to the lack of nearby infrastructure providing fuel, oxygen, and other supplies. Other corporations witnessed this and declined to imitate the attempt.

Over the centuries, the exact location of the base amidst the various other asteroids in the belt has been lost to the public and the name Glitter Gulch has faded into legend.
nodes.gmNotes: This abandoned location is listed as a potential hideout for the FFU, should they need a secret refuge in future.

nodes.name: Diamedes Orbital Research Outpost (Abandoned)
nodes.description:
A small, rotating habitation module designed for long-term scientific observation of a planetary body. Includes sensor arrays and a small centrifuge for gravity.

This orbital research outpost was originally built to allow science teams to gather data on the unusual environment on Diamedes. After a few decades funding for the research dried up and the facility was abandoned.
nodes.gmNotes: This abandoned location is listed as a potential hideout for the FFU, should they need a secret refuge in future.

nodes.name: Cerebus Alpha Orbital Research Outpost (Abandoned)
nodes.description:
A small, rotating habitation module designed for long-term scientific observation of a planetary body. Includes sensor arrays and a small centrifuge for gravity.

This facility was built centuries ago to study the volcanic activity seen on Cerebus A by a consortium of corporations hoping to leverage it, or at least learnings from it, to generate geothermal power. It was abandoned decades later and has seen little in the way of visitors since, being treated as a destination for curious adventurers.
nodes.gmNotes: This abandoned location is listed as a potential hideout for the FFU, should they need a secret refuge in future.

nodes.name: Sentry Two - Dragon Class System Defence Boat
nodes.description: Heavily armed and armoured, the 400 dTon Dragon class system defence boat is fully capable of standing toe-to-toe with much larger starships. Its design exchanges the considerable mass and volume consumed by a jump drive, replacing it with heavy armour, two particle barbettes, a type II point-defence laser system, and considerable missile storage for its small missile bay.
nodes.gmNotes:
This ship carries the following missile loadout in its small missile bay:

* 12 anti-torpedo missiles
* 24 fragmentation missiles
* 24 long-range missiles
* 84 standard missiles (initial set)

It can fire salvos of 12 missiles at a time.

Plus the following missiles in storage:

* 36 anti-torpedo missiles
* 72 fragmentation missiles
* 72 fragmentation missiles
* 300 standard missiles

nodes.name: Red Wing
nodes.description: This is a 400 dTon Gazelle close escort leading a wing of 4 10 dTon light fighters.

nodes.name: Blue Wing
nodes.description: This is a 400 dTon Gazelle close escort leading a wing of 4 10 dTon light fighters.

nodes.name: Erasmus - Chrysanthemum Class Destroyer Escort
nodes.description: A 1000 dTon Chrysanthemum class destroyer escort, with a skeleton crew in warm storage. This ship can respond to immediate local threats, but is not crewed or supplied for interstellar missions, a task which would likely require up to a week to complete.
nodes.gmNotes:
The ship has readied the following missile loadout:

* 1 triple turret with standard missiles (36 standard missiles)
* 1 triple turret with long range missiles (36 long range missiles)

Each turret can fire salvos of 3 missiles at a time.

Plus the following missiles in storage:
* 288 standard missiles
* 96 long range missiles

nodes.name: Pelagius - Chrysanthemum Class Destroyer Escort
nodes.description: A 1000 dTon Chrysanthemum class destroyer escort, with a skeleton crew in warm storage. This ship can respond to immediate local threats, but is not crewed or supplied for interstellar missions, a task which would likely require up to a week to complete.
nodes.gmNotes:
The ship has readied the following missile loadout:

* 1 triple turret with standard missiles (36 standard missiles)
* 1 triple turret with long range missiles (36 long range missiles)

Each turret can fire salvos of 3 missiles at a time.

Plus the following missiles in storage:
* 288 standard missiles
* 96 long range missiles

nodes.name: UISSS Inc. Shuttle Depot Alpha
nodes.description: A rotating station in a key orbital position, serving as the primary storage and maintenance depot for the Uggi Inner System Shuttle Service - UISSS - fleet of shuttles, which provides on-demand shuttle services throughout the inner Uggi system. Spin gravity is ~0.8G.
